description: PSR B1919+21 — the pulsar redrawn in 1979 onto one of the most-photocopied record covers in popular music — broadcasts at us. Every 1.337 seconds, when its rotation aligns, a 180-millisecond keyhole opens. We catch what survives the distance: a memory of a recording, not the recording itself. The cover redraws itself, one rotation at a time, in real time with the broadcast.
license_notes: IBM Plex Mono (SIL OFL 1.1) and EB Garamond (SIL OFL 1.1) loaded from Google Fonts. node-web-audio-api (BSD-3-Clause) used at build time only. The source recording is excluded from the repository; the rendered broadcast (transformative derivative — 180 ms windowed, heavily processed) is included so the runtime can play. See STATEMENT.txt in the project for the full attribution / pastiche rationale (§ 51a UrhG, EuGH Pelham C-476/17 / fair use).
